### CONTROL POINT: ACC-001
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Double Entry Enforcement
Purpose: Prevent ledger corruption by ensuring every journal remains balanced.
Rule: Total debit must equal total credit at transaction save and post time for all journal transactions.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under double entry enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces double entry enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the double entry enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- document-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- journal posting
- invoice posting
- payment posting
- adjustments
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- journal payload sample
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Foundational accounting integrity control point.

Additional Fields:
category_code:
ACC

name:
Double Entry Enforcement

measurable_fields:
[
  "rule_status",
  "actor",
  "timestamp",
  "source_reference"
]

audit_method:
manual

linked_workflows:
[
  "journal posting",
  "invoice posting",
  "payment posting",
  "adjustments"
]

master_design_reference:
{
  "nodeId": "double-entry-enforcement",
  "nodeTitle": "Double Entry Enforcement",
  "parentNodeId": "accounting-standards",
  "parentNodeTitle": "Accounting Standards",
  "path": [
    "Whole System",
    "Standards",
    "Accounting Standards",
    "Double Entry Enforcement"
  ]
}

category_mapping:
{
  "requestedCategory": "Accounting",
  "masterDesignCategory": "Accounting Standards",
  "source": "master-design-hierarchy"
}

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-002
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Accounting Equation Integrity
Purpose: Protect ledger-level balance-sheet truth after every posting cycle.
Rule: Postings must preserve Assets = Liabilities + Equity at ledger level.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under accounting equation integrity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces accounting equation integrity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-002 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-002 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-002 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the accounting equation integrity rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Accounting Equation Integrity is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Accounting Equation Integrity is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- reports-engine
Linked Processes:
- ledger rollup
- financial statement generation
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Accounting Equation Integrity is part of the Accounting standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-003
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Design
Title: Compound Entry Support
Purpose: Allow valid multi-line accounting transactions without breaking transaction unity.
Rule: A single transaction may validly contain three or more account lines and must remain one transaction.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal design behavior that falls under compound entry support.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces compound entry support consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-003 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-003 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-003 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the compound entry support rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative journal design scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Compound Entry Support is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Compound Entry Support is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- reports-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- journal drafting
- compound posting
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Accounting Core

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-004
Category: Accounting
Domain: Chart of Accounts
Title: Contra Account Polarity
Purpose: Ensure contra balances are modeled and reviewed with the correct opposite orientation.
Rule: Contra accounts must carry balance orientation opposite to parent classification.
Condition: When the system executes or validates chart of accounts behavior that falls under contra account polarity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces contra account polarity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-012
Category: Accounting
Domain: Balance Sheet Timing
Title: Deferred Revenue Handling
Purpose: Keep unearned revenue on the balance sheet until release conditions are met.
Rule: Advance-billed but not-yet-earned amounts must remain deferred until release conditions are met.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-013
Category: Accounting
Domain: Balance Sheet Timing
Title: Prepaid Expense Handling
Purpose: Keep prepaid assets from being recognized too early.
Rule: Prepaid amounts must remain on the balance sheet until recognized over time or use.
Condition: When the system executes or validates balance sheet timing behavior that falls under prepaid expense handling.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces prepaid expense handling consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-013 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-013 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-013 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the prepaid expense handling rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative balance sheet timing scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Prepaid Expense Handling is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Prepaid Expense Handling is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- reports-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:

- expense amortization
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Prepaid Expense Handling is part of the Accounting standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-014
Category: Accounting
Domain: Period Adjustments

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-016

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-017
Category: Accounting
Domain: Period Governance
Title: Period Closing Block
Purpose: Prevent financial period closure when integrity issues remain unresolved.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-018
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Governance
Title: Journal Immutability After Final Post
Purpose: Protect posted audit trails from destructive edits.
Rule: Finalized journals must not be destructively edited; only reversal or adjustment path is allowed.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal governance behavior that falls under journal immutability after final post.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces journal immutability after final post consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: INV-003
Category: Inventory
Domain: Stock Governance
Title: Negative Stock Exception Governance
Purpose: Ensure exception-based negative stock use is explicit and reviewable.
Rule: If negative stock is enabled, the event must be explicitly flagged and auditable.
Condition: When the system executes or validates stock governance behavior that falls under negative stock exception governance.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces negative stock exception governance consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- inv-003 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- inv-003 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- inv-003 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the negative stock exception governance rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative stock governance scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Negative Stock Exception Governance is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Negative Stock Exception Governance is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:

- accounting-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:

- negative stock logging

- structured payload

Category: Inventory
Domain: Adjustment Governance
Title: Inventory Adjustment Traceability
Purpose: Ensure manual inventory changes remain accountable when financial impact exists.
Rule: Manual adjustments must require reason, user, timestamp, and accounting linkage when financial impact exists.
Condition: When the system executes or validates adjustment governance behavior that falls under inventory adjustment traceability.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces inventory adjustment traceability consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- inv-015 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- inv-015 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- inv-015 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the inventory adjustment traceability rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative adjustment governance scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Inventory Adjustment Traceability is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Inventory Adjustment Traceability is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: partial
Linked Modules:
- inventory-engine
- accounting-engine
- proof-layer
Linked Processes:
- inventory adjustment
- adjustment approval
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Inventory Core
Notes: Inventory Adjustment Traceability is part of the Inventory standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-003

Domain: Recovery
Title: Empty-State CTA Requirement
Purpose: Ensure empty states remain actionable.
Rule: Empty states must include a relevant call to action.
Condition: When the system executes or validates recovery behavior that falls under empty-state cta requirement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces empty-state cta requirement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- uix-008 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- uix-008 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- uix-008 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the empty-state cta requirement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative recovery scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Empty-State CTA Requirement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Empty-State CTA Requirement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- workflow-intelligence
- identity-workspace
Linked Processes:
- empty register review
- first-action review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Experience Layer
Notes: Empty-State CTA Requirement is part of the UI / UX standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

Category: Input Validation / Data Standards
Domain: Address Validation
Title: Address Line Structured Fields
Purpose: Prevent business address data from collapsing into a single opaque free-text field.
Rule: Address capture must support structured address-line fields.
Condition: When the system executes or validates address validation behavior that falls under address line structured fields.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces address line structured fields consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- val-009 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- val-009 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- val-009 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the address line structured fields rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative address validation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Address Line Structured Fields is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Address Line Structured Fields is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:

- contacts-counterparties

- customer address capture

- structured payload

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-001
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Double Entry Enforcement
Purpose: Prevent ledger corruption by ensuring every journal remains balanced.
Rule: Total debit must equal total credit at transaction save and post time for all journal transactions.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under double entry enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces double entry enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the double entry enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- document-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- journal posting
- invoice posting
- payment posting
- adjustments
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- journal payload sample
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Foundational accounting integrity control point.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-002
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Accounting Equation Integrity
Purpose: Protect ledger-level balance-sheet truth after every posting cycle.
Rule: Postings must preserve Assets = Liabilities + Equity at ledger level.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under accounting equation integrity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces accounting equation integrity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-003
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Design
Title: Compound Entry Support
Purpose: Allow valid multi-line accounting transactions without breaking transaction unity.
Rule: A single transaction may validly contain three or more account lines and must remain one transaction.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal design behavior that falls under compound entry support.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces compound entry support consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-003 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-003 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-003 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the compound entry support rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative journal design scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-004
Category: Accounting
Domain: Chart of Accounts
Title: Contra Account Polarity
Purpose: Ensure contra balances are modeled and reviewed with the correct opposite orientation.
Rule: Contra accounts must carry balance orientation opposite to parent classification.
Condition: When the system executes or validates chart of accounts behavior that falls under contra account polarity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces contra account polarity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-005

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-012

Title: Deferred Revenue Handling

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-015
Category: Accounting
Domain: Period Matching
Title: Matching Principle Enforcement
Purpose: Support expense timing aligned with associated revenue periods when applicable.
Rule: Expense recognition should align to associated revenue period when applicable.
Condition: When the system executes or validates period matching behavior that falls under matching principle enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces matching principle enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-015 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-015 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-015 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the matching principle enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative period matching scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Matching Principle Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Matching Principle Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-017

Domain: Period Governance
Title: Period Closing Block

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-018

Title: Journal Immutability After Final Post

### CONTROL POINT: INV-005
Category: Inventory
Domain: Valuation
Title: FIFO Valuation Enforcement
Purpose: Keep inventory valuation aligned to FIFO as the core standard.
Rule: Inventory valuation must support FIFO as a core standard.
Condition: When the system executes or validates valuation behavior that falls under fifo valuation enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces fifo valuation enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- inv-005 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- inv-005 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- inv-005 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the fifo valuation enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative valuation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: FIFO Valuation Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: FIFO Valuation Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- inventory-engine
- accounting-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- cost layer consumption
- inventory valuation reporting
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Inventory Core
Notes: FIFO Valuation Enforcement is part of the Inventory standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-002
Category: VAT / Tax / Compliance
Domain: Tax Separation
Title: Input VAT Separation
Purpose: Keep recoverable purchase tax distinct from purchase net values.
Rule: Input VAT must be recorded separately from net purchase and expense values.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tax separation behavior that falls under input vat separation.

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-024
Category: VAT / Tax / Compliance
Domain: Structured Tax Data
Title: Structured Tax Data Completeness
Purpose: Keep tax-critical fields available in machine-readable form for downstream compliance use.
Rule: Tax-critical fields must exist in machine-readable structure, not only visual PDF text.
Condition: When the system executes or validates structured tax data behavior that falls under structured tax data completeness.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces structured tax data completeness consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- vat-024 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- vat-024 control evidence is missing or incomplete

### CONTROL POINT: FRM-011
Category: Forms / Registers
Domain: Persistence UX
Title: Unsaved Change Protection
Purpose: Protect operators from losing in-progress work.
Rule: Forms must protect against silent loss of unsaved critical changes.

Purpose: Ensure fax inputs, when used, follow an expected structure.
Rule: Fax validation must enforce supported format.
Condition: When the system executes or validates contact validation behavior that falls under fax format rule.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces fax format rule consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- val-011 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- val-011 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- val-011 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the fax format rule rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative contact validation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Fax Format Rule is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Fax Format Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-005
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Access Control
Title: RBAC Enforcement
Purpose: Ensure roles and permissions are actually enforced, not just documented.
Rule: System must enforce role-based access control across protected actions.
Condition: When the system executes or validates access control behavior that falls under rbac enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces rbac enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- sec-005 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- sec-005 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- sec-005 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the rbac enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative access control scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: RBAC Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: RBAC Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- identity-workspace
- ui-ux-shell
- proof-layer
Linked Processes:
- role gating
- permission enforcement
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Security and Governance
Notes: RBAC Enforcement is part of the Security / Audit standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

Title: Credit Note Accounting Link

- xmd-004 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- xmd-004 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows

- Execute or simulate a representative cross-module traceability scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Credit Note Accounting Link is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Credit Note Accounting Link is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-001
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Double Entry Enforcement
Purpose: Prevent ledger corruption by ensuring every journal remains balanced.
Rule: Total debit must equal total credit at transaction save and post time for all journal transactions.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under double entry enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces double entry enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the double entry enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- document-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- journal posting
- invoice posting
- payment posting
- adjustments
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- journal payload sample
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Foundational accounting integrity control point.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-002
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Accounting Equation Integrity
Purpose: Protect ledger-level balance-sheet truth after every posting cycle.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-003
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Design
Title: Compound Entry Support
Purpose: Allow valid multi-line accounting transactions without breaking transaction unity.
Rule: A single transaction may validly contain three or more account lines and must remain one transaction.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal design behavior that falls under compound entry support.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces compound entry support consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-004

Title: Contra Account Polarity

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-005
Category: Accounting
Domain: Commercial Posting
Title: Discount Allowed Posting
Purpose: Ensure discounts granted to customers remain visible in financial reporting.
Rule: Discount allowed must post to dedicated expense or revenue-reduction logic and must not disappear silently.
Condition: When the system executes or validates commercial posting behavior that falls under discount allowed posting.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces discount allowed posting consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

Severity: high
Priority: immediate

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-012

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-017
Category: Accounting
Domain: Period Governance
Title: Period Closing Block
Purpose: Prevent financial period closure when integrity issues remain unresolved.
Rule: Close or lock period must fail if trial balance or posting integrity issues remain.
Condition: When the system executes or validates period governance behavior that falls under period closing block.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces period closing block consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-017 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-017 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-017 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the period closing block rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative period governance scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-018
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Governance
Title: Journal Immutability After Final Post
Purpose: Protect posted audit trails from destructive edits.
Rule: Finalized journals must not be destructively edited; only reversal or adjustment path is allowed.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal governance behavior that falls under journal immutability after final post.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces journal immutability after final post consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-018 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-018 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-018 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:

- Execute or simulate a representative journal governance scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Journal Immutability After Final Post is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Journal Immutability After Final Post is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: partial

- reports-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:

- reversal workflow

- structured payload

Purpose: Keep stock movement and financial impact aligned across modules.
Rule: Inventory asset, COGS, adjustment, and stock movement must reconcile with the accounting engine.

Expected Behavior: The system enforces inventory-to-accounting synchronization consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

- inv-007 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-004

Domain: Tax Decision Engine
Title: Customer Origin Capture Dependency
Purpose: Make tax decisions depend on customer origin where required by regime logic.

Condition: When the system executes or validates tax decision engine behavior that falls under customer origin capture dependency.

### CONTROL POINT: DOC-002

Purpose: Keep previewed business truth aligned with exported PDF output.

### CONTROL POINT: UIX-001
Category: UI / UX
Domain: Comprehension
Title: 5-Second Understanding Rule
Purpose: Ensure operators can understand the page purpose almost immediately.
Rule: Primary workspace surfaces must communicate purpose and action within five seconds.
Condition: When the system executes or validates comprehension behavior that falls under 5-second understanding rule.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces 5-second understanding rule consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- uix-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- uix-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- uix-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the 5-second understanding rule rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative comprehension scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: 5-Second Understanding Rule is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: 5-Second Understanding Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- identity-workspace
Linked Processes:
- workspace landing
- page comprehension review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Experience Layer
Notes: 5-Second Understanding Rule is part of the UI / UX standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: UIX-003
Category: UI / UX
Domain: Recovery
Title: No Dead-End Screens
Purpose: Avoid inert screens that leave users without a next action.
Rule: Screens must not become dead ends when blocked, empty, or partially available.
Condition: When the system executes or validates recovery behavior that falls under no dead-end screens.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces no dead-end screens consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-007
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Audit Logging
Title: Audit Log User Identity
Purpose: Ensure audit entries identify the acting user.

Domain: State Consistency
Title: Document Status vs Accounting State Consistency
Purpose: Prevent documents and accounting state from drifting apart.
Rule: Document status must remain consistent with resulting accounting state.
Condition: When the system executes or validates state consistency behavior that falls under document status vs accounting state consistency.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces document status vs accounting state consistency consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- xmd-006 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- xmd-006 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- xmd-006 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the document status vs accounting state consistency rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative state consistency scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Document Status vs Accounting State Consistency is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Document Status vs Accounting State Consistency is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- document-engine
- accounting-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- status transition review
- posting state review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Platform Integrity
Notes: Document Status vs Accounting State Consistency is part of the Cross-Module / System Integrity standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

XMD

Document Status vs Accounting State Consistency

]

]

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-001
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Double Entry Enforcement
Purpose: Prevent ledger corruption by ensuring every journal remains balanced.
Rule: Total debit must equal total credit at transaction save and post time for all journal transactions.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under double entry enforcement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces double entry enforcement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the double entry enforcement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Double Entry Enforcement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- accounting-engine
- document-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- journal posting
- invoice posting
- payment posting
- adjustments
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- journal payload sample
Owner: Accounting Core
Notes: Foundational accounting integrity control point.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-002
Category: Accounting
Domain: Ledger Integrity
Title: Accounting Equation Integrity
Purpose: Protect ledger-level balance-sheet truth after every posting cycle.
Rule: Postings must preserve Assets = Liabilities + Equity at ledger level.
Condition: When the system executes or validates ledger integrity behavior that falls under accounting equation integrity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces accounting equation integrity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

- acc-002 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows

- Execute or simulate a representative ledger integrity scenario and verify the rule is enforced.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-003
Category: Accounting
Domain: Journal Design
Title: Compound Entry Support
Purpose: Allow valid multi-line accounting transactions without breaking transaction unity.
Rule: A single transaction may validly contain three or more account lines and must remain one transaction.
Condition: When the system executes or validates journal design behavior that falls under compound entry support.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces compound entry support consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:

- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

Fail Criteria: Compound Entry Support is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready

- workflow-intelligence

- compound posting

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-004
Category: Accounting
Domain: Chart of Accounts
Title: Contra Account Polarity
Purpose: Ensure contra balances are modeled and reviewed with the correct opposite orientation.
Rule: Contra accounts must carry balance orientation opposite to parent classification.

Category: Accounting
Domain: Commercial Posting
Title: Discount Allowed Posting
Purpose: Ensure discounts granted to customers remain visible in financial reporting.
Rule: Discount allowed must post to dedicated expense or revenue-reduction logic and must not disappear silently.

### CONTROL POINT: ACC-006
Category: Accounting
Domain: Commercial Posting
Title: Discount Received Posting
Purpose: Ensure supplier-side discounts are recorded through explicit accounting treatment.
Rule: Discount received must post to dedicated income or cost-reduction logic.
Condition: When the system executes or validates commercial posting behavior that falls under discount received posting.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces discount received posting consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- acc-006 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- acc-006 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- acc-006 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the discount received posting rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative commercial posting scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Discount Received Posting is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Discount Received Posting is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready

### CONTROL POINT: INV-003
Category: Inventory
Domain: Stock Governance
Title: Negative Stock Exception Governance
Purpose: Ensure exception-based negative stock use is explicit and reviewable.
Rule: If negative stock is enabled, the event must be explicitly flagged and auditable.
Condition: When the system executes or validates stock governance behavior that falls under negative stock exception governance.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces negative stock exception governance consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

- inv-003 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- inv-003 control evidence is missing or incomplete

- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

Fail Criteria: Negative Stock Exception Governance is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.

### CONTROL POINT: INV-012
Category: Inventory
Domain: Inventory Planning
Title: Safety Stock Threshold
Purpose: Enable minimum-stock governance before stock-outs occur.
Rule: Reorder and alert logic must support minimum stock buffer.
Condition: When the system executes or validates inventory planning behavior that falls under safety stock threshold.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces safety stock threshold consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- inv-012 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- inv-012 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- inv-012 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the safety stock threshold rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative inventory planning scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Safety Stock Threshold is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Safety Stock Threshold is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- inventory-engine
- accounting-engine
- workflow-intelligence
Linked Processes:
- reorder alerting
- stock risk review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Inventory Core
Notes: Safety Stock Threshold is part of the Inventory standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-001
Category: VAT / Tax / Compliance
Domain: Tax Separation
Title: Output VAT Separation
Purpose: Keep output tax distinct from net sales value.
Rule: Output VAT must be recorded separately from net sales values.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tax separation behavior that falls under output vat separation.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces output vat separation consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- vat-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- vat-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- vat-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows

- Execute or simulate a representative tax separation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Output VAT Separation is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Output VAT Separation is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented

- tax-vat-engine
- accounting-engine
- document-engine

- invoice presentation

### CONTROL POINT: VAT-023
Category: VAT / Tax / Compliance
Domain: EU E-Invoicing
Title: EN 16931 Semantic Mapping
Purpose: Prepare invoice structured data for EU semantic interoperability.

Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- document-engine
- compliance-layer

### CONTROL POINT: DOC-009
Category: Documents
Domain: Pagination
Title: Multi-Page Continuity
Purpose: Maintain numbering and layout continuity across multi-page documents.
Rule: Multi-page documents must preserve numbering and layout continuity.
Condition: When the system executes or validates pagination behavior that falls under multi-page continuity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces multi-page continuity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- doc-009 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- doc-009 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- doc-009 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the multi-page continuity rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative pagination scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Multi-Page Continuity is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Multi-Page Continuity is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- document-engine
- template-engine
- compliance-layer
Linked Processes:
- pagination
- multi-page print review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Document Core
Notes: Multi-Page Continuity is part of the Documents standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: UIX-002
Category: UI / UX
Domain: Task Efficiency
Title: 5-Click Completion Rule
Purpose: Keep common workflows operationally short.
Rule: Common business tasks should complete within five logical clicks when preconditions are satisfied.
Condition: When the system executes or validates task efficiency behavior that falls under 5-click completion rule.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces 5-click completion rule consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- uix-002 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- uix-002 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- uix-002 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:

- Execute or simulate a representative task efficiency scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: 5-Click Completion Rule is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: 5-Click Completion Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- workflow-intelligence
- identity-workspace
Linked Processes:
- task path review
- workflow optimization baseline
Evidence Required:

- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Experience Layer
Notes: 5-Click Completion Rule is part of the UI / UX standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: UIX-003

### CONTROL POINT: VAL-016
Category: Input Validation / Data Standards
Domain: Presentation Validation
Title: Comma Grouping / Number Presentation Rule
Purpose: Keep visible numeric formatting readable and predictable.
Rule: Number presentation rules must support readable grouping without altering stored numeric truth.
Condition: When the system executes or validates presentation validation behavior that falls under comma grouping / number presentation rule.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces comma grouping / number presentation rule consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- val-016 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- val-016 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- val-016 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the comma grouping / number presentation rule rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative presentation validation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Comma Grouping / Number Presentation Rule is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Comma Grouping / Number Presentation Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- company-profile
- contacts-counterparties
Linked Processes:
- numeric formatting
- document number presentation
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Validation Layer
Notes: Comma Grouping / Number Presentation Rule is part of the Input Validation / Data Standards standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: VAL-017
Category: Input Validation / Data Standards
Domain: Validation UX
Title: Inline Guidance Requirement
Purpose: Ensure users receive format guidance before they hit preventable validation errors.
Rule: Relevant validation fields must expose inline guidance where structure is not obvious.
Condition: When the system executes or validates validation ux behavior that falls under inline guidance requirement.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces inline guidance requirement consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- val-017 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- val-017 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- val-017 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the inline guidance requirement rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative validation ux scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Inline Guidance Requirement is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Inline Guidance Requirement is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- company-profile
- contacts-counterparties
Linked Processes:
- field hint rendering
- input guidance
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Validation Layer
Notes: Inline Guidance Requirement is part of the Input Validation / Data Standards standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: VAL-018
Category: Input Validation / Data Standards
Domain: Validation UX
Title: Submission Blocking Rule
Purpose: Ensure critical invalid data cannot pass through save or submit flows.
Rule: Submission must block when critical validation rules fail.
Condition: When the system executes or validates validation ux behavior that falls under submission blocking rule.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces submission blocking rule consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- val-018 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- val-018 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- val-018 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the submission blocking rule rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative validation ux scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Submission Blocking Rule is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Submission Blocking Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: implemented
Linked Modules:
- ui-ux-shell
- company-profile
- contacts-counterparties
Linked Processes:
- submit gating
- critical validation enforcement
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Validation Layer
Notes: Submission Blocking Rule is part of the Input Validation / Data Standards standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-001
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Tenant Isolation
Title: Tenant Resource Ownership Check
Purpose: Ensure records belong to the tenant context that is attempting access.
Rule: Every protected resource access must verify tenant resource ownership.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tenant isolation behavior that falls under tenant resource ownership check.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces tenant resource ownership check consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- sec-001 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- sec-001 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- sec-001 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the tenant resource ownership check rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative tenant isolation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Tenant Resource Ownership Check is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Tenant Resource Ownership Check is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- identity-workspace
- proof-layer
- compliance-layer
Linked Processes:
- resource fetch
- resource mutation
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Security and Governance
Notes: Tenant Resource Ownership Check is part of the Security / Audit standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-002
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Tenant Isolation
Title: User Belongs to Tenant Check
Purpose: Ensure the acting user is attached to the tenant context they are using.
Rule: Every protected action must verify that the user belongs to the active tenant.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tenant isolation behavior that falls under user belongs to tenant check.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces user belongs to tenant check consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- sec-002 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- sec-002 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- sec-002 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the user belongs to tenant check rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative tenant isolation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: User Belongs to Tenant Check is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: User Belongs to Tenant Check is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- identity-workspace
- proof-layer
- compliance-layer
Linked Processes:
- session validation
- tenant membership validation
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Security and Governance
Notes: User Belongs to Tenant Check is part of the Security / Audit standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-003
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Tenant Isolation
Title: Role Allows Action Check
Purpose: Ensure role and permission checks complete the triple-check isolation rule.
Rule: Every protected action must verify the active role allows the requested action.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tenant isolation behavior that falls under role allows action check.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces role allows action check consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- sec-003 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path

- sec-003 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows

- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

Fail Criteria: Role Allows Action Check is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global

- structured payload

### CONTROL POINT: SEC-004
Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Tenant Isolation
Title: Cross-Tenant Leak Prevention
Purpose: Prevent accidental or unauthorized data exposure across tenant boundaries.
Rule: System must prevent cross-tenant data leaks in reads, writes, exports, and logs.
Condition: When the system executes or validates tenant isolation behavior that falls under cross-tenant leak prevention.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces cross-tenant leak prevention consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- sec-004 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- sec-004 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- sec-004 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the cross-tenant leak prevention rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative tenant isolation scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Cross-Tenant Leak Prevention is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Cross-Tenant Leak Prevention is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: critical
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- identity-workspace
- proof-layer
- compliance-layer
Linked Processes:
- tenant-bound query review
- cross-tenant export review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Security and Governance

- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.

Category: Security / Audit
Domain: Audit Logging
Title: Audit Log User Identity
Purpose: Ensure audit entries identify the acting user.

Condition: When the system executes or validates audit logging behavior that falls under audit log user identity.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces audit log user identity consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

- sec-007 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows

- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Audit Log User Identity is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Audit Log User Identity is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global

Title: 90-Day Hot Retention

Condition: When the system executes or validates retention behavior that falls under 90-day hot retention.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces 90-day hot retention consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.

Severity: high

### CONTROL POINT: BRD-001
Category: Branding / Identity Standards
Domain: Brand Asset Governance
Title: Full Wordmark Usage Rule
Purpose: Ensure the full Gulf Hisab wordmark is used wherever full brand identity is required.
Rule: Use Gulf Hisab SVG Logo 1 where full brand identity is required.

Fail Criteria: Full Wordmark Usage Rule is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.

Fail Criteria: Invoice-to-Journal Link is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.

--------------------------------------------------

### CONTROL POINT: XMD-010
Category: Cross-Module / System Integrity
Domain: State Consistency
Title: Register / Detail / Print Data Consistency
Purpose: Ensure the same business truth appears in lists, details, and printed outputs.
Rule: Register, detail, and print surfaces must remain consistent for the same record.
Condition: When the system executes or validates state consistency behavior that falls under register / detail / print data consistency.
Expected Behavior: The system enforces register / detail / print data consistency consistently and emits auditable evidence whenever the rule is evaluated.
Failure Conditions:
- xmd-010 rule is bypassed in a primary transaction path
- xmd-010 control evidence is missing or incomplete
- xmd-010 behavior differs between interactive and downstream flows
Audit Steps:
- Inspect the register / detail / print data consistency rule in the relevant module and supporting process configuration.
- Execute or simulate a representative state consistency scenario and verify the rule is enforced.
- Review resulting evidence and confirm the outcome matches the expected control behavior.
Pass Criteria: Register / Detail / Print Data Consistency is enforced across the mapped modules and processes with traceable evidence.
Fail Criteria: Register / Detail / Print Data Consistency is missing, bypassed, inconsistent, or cannot be evidenced in the mapped flows.
Severity: high
Priority: immediate
Applicability: KSA, Gulf, EU, Global
Implementation Status: future_ready
Linked Modules:
- document-engine
- ui-ux-shell
- template-engine
Linked Processes:
- register review
- detail review
- print review
Evidence Required:
- test result
- validation log
- structured payload
Owner: Platform Integrity
Notes: Register / Detail / Print Data Consistency is part of the Cross-Module / System Integrity standards layer and is prepared for later audit-engine binding.
